header.title: Number Base Converter — Binary, Octal, Decimal & Hex Online
header.description: Free online number base converter to convert numbers between binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal, and any base from 2 to 36. Instantly translate between numbering systems with step-by-step conversion.
header.keywords: number base converter, binary converter, octal converter, hexadecimal converter, base converter, decimal to binary, hex to decimal, number system converter

allBases.title: All Bases
allBases.enterToConvert: Enter a number above to convert

arithmetic.title: Base Arithmetic
arithmetic.description: Perform addition, subtraction, multiplication and division in any base:

reference.title: Quick Reference (0–15)
reference.clickRow: Click any row to load that value

howToUse.title: How to Use Number Base Converter
howToUse.step1Desc: Type the number you want to convert in the input field.
howToUse.step2Desc: Choose the base of your input number from the dropdown, ranging from binary (2) to base 36.
howToUse.step3Desc: Pick the target base for conversion. All other bases update instantly.
howToUse.step4Desc: View bit width, byte segments, LSB, MSB position, and whether the number is a power of two.

items.question: What number bases are supported?
items.answer: The converter supports all bases from 2 (binary) to 36, including octal (8), decimal (10), and hexadecimal (16). You can also convert between any two custom bases.

items.question: What happens if I enter an invalid character for the selected base?
items.answer: The tool will highlight invalid characters and prevent conversion. For example, entering "1G" in base 16 is invalid because G is not a hexadecimal digit (0-9 and A-F only).

items.question: How does custom base conversion work?
items.answer: Select "Custom" from the base dropdown and enter your desired base number (2-36). The converter will handle the conversion using standard radix algorithms.

items.question: What is bit analysis used for?
items.answer: Bit analysis shows the binary representation of your number, including bit width, number of set and zero bits, LSB and MSB positions, and whether the number is a power of two. Useful for low-level programming and digital logic.

items.question: Can I perform arithmetic in different bases?
items.answer: Yes, enable "Base Arithmetic" to perform addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division directly in any base. Results are shown in the same base.

items.question: What characters are used for bases above 10?
items.answer: Base 11 to 36 use digits 0-9 followed by letters A-Z. For example, base 16 uses 0-9 and A-F, while base 36 uses 0-9 and A-Z.
